output.summary: Set hero minimum height to min(700px, 100vh) and align content to the bottom using flexbox. Pure CSS/layout change — no attribute or block.json changes needed.

fileChanges.description: Add min-height and flex bottom-alignment to hero-inner and content layer
fileChanges.details: 1. On the `.hero-inner` div: add `min-h-[min(700px,100vh)]`, `flex`, `flex-col`, and `items-end` (or use a child wrapper with `mt-auto`). 2. On the content layer div: replace the large top-padding approach (`pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+180px)]` etc.) with `mt-auto` so content sits at the bottom. Keep `pb-8 sm:pb-16` for bottom spacing. 3. Add a top padding of `pt-[var(--header-height)]` as a minimum safeguard so content never overlaps the header on very short viewports.

fileChanges.description: Mirror the same min-height and flex bottom-alignment changes from TSX
fileChanges.details: 1. On the `.hero-inner` div: add `min-h-[min(700px,100vh)] flex flex-col`. 2. On the content layer div: replace `pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+180px)] sm:pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+250px)]` (primary) and `pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+180px)] sm:pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+280px)]` (secondary) with `mt-auto pt-[var(--header-height)]`. Keep `pb-8 sm:pb-16`.

designDecisions.decision: Use min-h-[min(700px,100vh)] on .hero-inner as the minimum height
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested min(700px, 100vh) as the hero minimum height

designDecisions.decision: Use flexbox with mt-auto on the content layer to bottom-align content
designDecisions.evidence: User requested content aligned to the bottom. Flexbox column with mt-auto is the standard Tailwind pattern for bottom alignment within a min-height container.

designDecisions.decision: Replace large fixed top-padding with mt-auto and a minimal pt-[var(--header-height)] safeguard
designDecisions.evidence: The current large pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+180px)] values were used to push content down. With flex bottom-alignment, content naturally sits at the bottom, so the large padding is no longer needed. A minimal header-height padding prevents overlap on short viewports.

output.notes: Both variations (primary and secondary) share the same hero-inner wrapper, so the min-height and flex changes apply to both. The primary variation has the QuickNavigation inner block below the ThemeHeading — ensure mt-auto is on the content layer (not QuickNav) so QuickNav still flows below the heading naturally. The background image mask and gradient overlay are unaffected since they use absolute positioning.
